text:
Market today: Chip stocks started Q3 on a weak note after a big Q2 run - that headline is the real driver for your book. Rotation and profit-taking in semis is the mood; your leveraged chip names feel this hardest.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the one screaming for attention - a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF already deep in the red, margin makes this exponentially more painful on further drops; stop at $200 is your line. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF (essentially an IPO-risk product) down nearly 14% with a razor-thin stop at $21.38 - one bad candle triggers it. SMH (-2.5%) is softer but less urgent; stop at $540 gives room.

Watch: Whether chip stocks stabilize or extend the Q3 dud - SMH price action is your tell. Also watch if Meta's cloud pivot sucks rotation money OUT of semiconductor pure-plays.

Bottom line: Your two leveraged losers (SOXL, SPAL) are the only real job today - honor those stops, margin leaves zero room for hoping.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is non-negotiable on margin - if you're not comfortable with that drawdown continuing, trim now rather than waiting for the stop to get hit in a fast move.
SPAL: $21.38 stop is inches away - this leveraged ETF decays and moves violently; consider cutting proactively given the -13.7% hole and the weak chip tape today.
No new buys today - chip sector starting Q3 soft, your leveraged positions need triage first; adding exposure here would be fighting the tape with borrowed money.
